title: CommuteBuddy BLE Message Schema
description: Documents the BLE message payloads exchanged between the Android companion app and the Garmin Connect IQ watch app via the Connect IQ Mobile SDK.

phoneToWatch.description: Message sent from Android app to Garmin watch via ConnectIQ.sendMessage()

steelThread.description: Steel-thread payload — a single integer representing the 4-digit test code. Used by FEAT-01 only.
notes: Sent as a plain Java Integer (not wrapped in a List or Dictionary).
The Connect IQ SDK serializes this to ~4 bytes on the wire — well under the 1KB BLE payload limit.
On the watch side, received as a Number in the Communications.registerForPhoneAppMessages() callback.

commuteStatus.description: Commute status payload — a minimized JSON object produced by on-device Gemini Nano summarization of raw MTA alert text.

status.description: 0 = Normal (good service), 1 = Deviate (delays/planned work), 2 = Error (major disruption or suspended service)

reason.description: Short human-readable reason for the status

timestamp.description: Unix epoch timestamp (seconds) of when this status was generated

notes: Sent as a Dictionary via ConnectIQ.sendMessage() — the Connect IQ SDK handles serialization.
On the watch side, received as a Dictionary in the Communications.registerForPhoneAppMessages() callback.
Total serialized size must stay well under the 1KB BLE payload limit.
The Android app validates and deserializes the Gemini Nano output into a Kotlin data class before sending.
If AI output fails validation, the phone retries or falls back to status=2 with reason='Parse error'.
Garmin Glance memory budget is approximately 32KB total; BLE message payloads must stay under 1KB.
Never send protobuf or large binary blobs — parse heavy data on Android and send only the result.
Never parse JSON on the watch — the Android app sends a pre-structured Dictionary via the Connect IQ SDK.
AI summarization (Gemini Nano) runs on-device on Android; no network call for the LLM step.
